target: HNOTE-OPERATOR-MOND-GROUPING-VERDICT-REVIEW-001 — three decisions: (1) verdict reclassification for five WINs (047/048/052/054/055), (2) placement of the cross-cutting argument, (3) operator-drafted section revisions. Cross-references operator research note at monitor/analyst/research-notes-MOND-grouping-2026-05-09.md.

decision_1_verdict_reclassification.operator_proposal: Reclassify WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055 from current verdict to Self-Contradicted under category-error / closed-world framework reading.
decision_1_verdict_reclassification.rationale: The five candidate WINs already contain framework-level contradiction language in their detail_evidence prose. WIN-052 explicitly states "the dome model’s physical structure (a 20,015 km disc enclosed by a firmament peaking at 9,086 km) cannot accommodate galaxies." WIN-054 explicitly presents the "inescapable dilemma" structure: either galaxies exist at the claimed distances (falsifying dome geometry) or they are illusions (in which case their measured properties cannot serve as evidence). WIN-048 already invokes "Self-contradiction with WIN-034" inline in its detail prose. WIN-047 says "no spatial framework for extragalactic objects — without one, any claim about their redshift behavior is physically undefined." WIN-055 says "10–100 Mpc — orders of magnitude beyond the dome’s firmament at 9,086 km." In each case the verdict label (Misleading or Not Demonstrated) trails what the prose already argues. Reclassification is bringing the label up to match the existing argument, not introducing a new framework reading.

counter_argument_a_addressed.operator_concern: The dome model may be silent rather than explicitly denying galaxies, in which case Misleading is technically correct.
counter_argument_a_addressed.analyst_response: REJECTED on the evidence. The dome’s bounded-firmament commitments are EXPLICIT (firmament_height = 9,086 km is a published parameter that propagates throughout the model and is invoked by the dome itself in WIN-029, WIN-053, WIN-067, and elsewhere). Silence on "galaxies as 3D mass distributions" plus explicit bounded-firmament parameters at ~10^7 m is not silence — it is positive contradiction. The dome cannot publish a maximum spatial extent of 30,000 km and simultaneously claim measurements at 10^25 m as confirming evidence without contradicting itself.
counter_argument_a_addressed.verification: Confirmed against current dome site by reading killshot.html and the wins.html parameter quotes. Existing data/sections.json prose at part6 already cites the bounded-firmament commitment from the dome’s coordinates.html.

counter_argument_b_addressed.operator_concern: Self-Contradicted has historically been applied to specific numerical/textual conflicts, not framework-level coherence; broadening it dilutes the verdict’s specificity.
counter_argument_b_addressed.analyst_response: PARTIALLY VALID but does not block reclassification. Survey of the 15 current Self-Contradicted WINs (002, 012, 014, 029, 038, 044, 045, 046, 049, 050, 051, 053, 056, 061, 067) shows the verdict has been applied for two patterns: (1) numerical/textual conflicts where dome’s own formulas/numbers don’t produce the dome’s claimed value (e.g., WIN-038 "dome’s own cavity cannot produce 7.83 Hz at any radius"); and (2) structural-coherence failures where the dome’s commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome’s own terms (e.g., WIN-053 "dome’s fitted two-pole formula violates flux conservation in its own claimed ring-magnet topology"; WIN-067 "dome’s own height formula predicts 90% gravity loss at the Antarctic rim, observed essentially zero"). The five candidates fit pattern (2). This is not broadening; it is the same pattern WIN-053 / WIN-067 already use. Mitigation against true dilution: each reclassified WIN gets sub_rationale "category-error import (MOND grouping)" so the framework-level basis is explicit and not conflated with numerical-conflict basis.

counter_argument_c_addressed.operator_concern: Revision-visibility cost — outside readers may see flipping five verdicts in flight as goalpost-moving.
counter_argument_c_addressed.analyst_response: REAL CONCERN, addressed by transparency. The Part 6.13 subsection (Decision 2 Option D) provides the explicit rationale and audit trail. The WIN-level detail_evidence prose for each of the five was already arguing SC-tier before this reclassification — reviewers can verify by reading the existing prose alongside the verdict-label change and confirm the prose did not move. Goalpost-moving requires moving the standard; here the standard (Self-Contradicted = dome’s own commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome’s own terms) is unchanged — the label is being aligned with the prose that already met the standard. Recommended additional mitigations: (1) record the five reclassifications in a single dated changelog entry naming all five at once and citing this EXP and the Part 6.13 subsection; (2) keep the operator research note as the public audit trail of the analytical scaffolding.

decision_2_placement.operator_preference: Option D (reclassify AND add a Part 6 subsection)
decision_2_placement.rationale: Option A (verdict change only, no separate section) is insufficient because the cross-cutting nature of the argument needs a single-place explanation. Option B (new verdict category like "Category-Error") dilutes the existing taxonomy when the existing Self-Contradicted definition already covers the pattern. Option C (no verdict change, only a section) creates divergence between the cross-cutting argument and the per-WIN tallies. Option D (both) gives the per-WIN labels their accurate reading AND provides a single-place audit trail.
decision_2_placement.placement_choice: Part 6.13 — The MOND Grouping: A Category-Error Import
decision_2_placement.why_part6: Part 6 is "Predictions Page — Structural Analysis" and already contains 12 cross-cutting subsections about patterns in how the dome’s registry is constructed (e.g., 6.4 Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions; 6.6b De-Duplication Analysis; 6.9 Structural Pattern: The Predictions Page as a Whole; 6.12 The April 2026 Closure Cluster). The MOND grouping is exactly that kind of structural / pattern observation. Inserting as 6.13 maintains chronological continuity in Part 6’s growth and matches the operator’s "between analyst and curmudgeon" framing.
decision_2_placement.why_not_part3: Part 3 is per-WIN analysis. The cross-cutting argument is a structural observation about a sub-set of WINs as a group, not a per-WIN treatment.
decision_2_placement.why_not_new_dedicated_tab: Over-weighting. The MOND grouping is one structural pattern among twelve already in Part 6; promoting it to its own tab would imply parity with whole-section topics like "Self-Contradictions," which is excessive for a sub-structural observation.

decision_3_section_revisions.operator_concern: TLDR may be too dense — carries (a) convergence-vs-import framing, (b) placement-choice / self-own observation, AND (c) closed-world conclusion.
decision_3_section_revisions.analyst_recommendation: TAKE THE OPERATOR-OFFERED EASY FIX: drop the placement-choice preview from the TLDR and let it land in the body. The TLDR now reads cleanly: convergence-vs-import + closed-world. The placement-choice argument lives in its dedicated subsection of the body where it can carry the necessary nuance.

critical_revision_required.issue: The operator’s drafted "placement-choice / self-own" section frames the kill-shot tab as "anti-globe ammunition" / "the canonical ammunition the dome author’s kill-shots tab is built to collect." This is FACTUALLY WRONG against the actual contents of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/killshot.html.
critical_revision_required.actual_kill_shot_tab_contents: The dome’s killshot.html page is a side-by-side discriminating-test table with six rows, each pairing a dome numerical prediction with a globe numerical prediction and a measured outcome (Sydney-Perth rail distance: 4,352 km vs 3,287 km; Polaris elevation at 35.9 deg N: 36.18 deg vs 35.91 deg; August 2026 eclipse magnetic at Ebro: -28.9 nT vs 0 nT; NMP drift rate by 2027: <20 km/yr vs no specific globe prediction; SAA African cell: <21,500 nT vs ~21,800 nT; JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream: eastbound >5% advantage vs 0%). The tab’s own footer rule says: "A clean result only counts as a major discriminator if the test is genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation." The tab is structured for items where dome and globe make different numerical predictions and one matches data — NOT for "things mainstream cosmology can’t explain."
critical_revision_required.consequence_if_unfixed: A dome defender who clicks killshot.html will immediately see the section’s premise is wrong. The "MOND items would have belonged in the kill-shots tab" claim is unsupported by the tab’s actual structure — the tab requires a dome-side numerical prediction, which the MOND items conspicuously lack (the dome’s rho_A = g_0 * rho_bar / g_dagger formula in WIN-052 has no numerical values for any of its symbols). Building the section’s rhetorical climax on a verifiably-false premise lets a defender legitimately dismiss the argument.
critical_revision_required.kernel_the_operator_was_right_about: The operator’s INSTINCT is correct: the dome’s claim taxonomy has multiple categories, and the MOND items were filed in the strongest-claim category (WIN = "confirmed prediction") for material that does not warrant that classification. The dome even has an internal sub-category — "LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction" — that more accurately describes the argument structure ("the alternative is in trouble, not my model is right"); WIN-048 is explicitly labeled with that sub-category in its detail prose. Calling out the placement choice is correct; pointing at the kill-shot tab as the alternative home is wrong.
critical_revision_required.proposed_fix: Rewrite the placement-choice paragraph to use the dome’s ACTUAL taxonomy (WINs, Predictions/Tracking, Kill-Shot side-by-side discriminating tests, internal "LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label inside WINs). The strong form of the self-own argument: even with the LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction sub-label available, and even though the author used it for WIN-048, the rest of the MOND-grouping items were filed as confirmed WINs claiming dome support. The author chose the strongest available claim category for material that does not warrant it. The kill-shot tab is unrelated to this argument because the kill-shot tab requires a dome-side numerical prediction (which the MOND items lack), not just an anti-mainstream observation.
critical_revision_required.replacement_section_html: See proposed_subsection_html field below; the placement-choice section has been rewritten to use the dome’s actual taxonomy and to make the self-own argument tighter rather than weaker.

other_edits.location: Operator-drafted body, "either galaxies are real and the firmament boundary is gone, or galaxies are firmament features and the imports cannot serve as evidence" dilemma
other_edits.analyst_recommendation: KEEP as-is. This is the rhetorical anchor of the closed-world section. Any tightening risks losing the explicit "two cannot both be held" structure that justifies the Self-Contradicted reclassification.

other_edits.location: Operator-drafted body, deeper-lesson section closing on "Even the fringe of real science precludes the dome."
other_edits.operator_offered: rhetorically hot; clinical close available if preferred
other_edits.analyst_recommendation: TRIM by one sentence. Drop the universal-quantifier close. Replace with "The neighborhood does not exist." (which is already the previous-paragraph close). The "fringe of real science precludes the dome" line is rhetorically loaded and gives a defender an opening to challenge the literal claim. The "neighborhood does not exist" close is already strong, doesn’t require defending a universal-quantifier claim, and lands on the deeper observation about scale mismatch that drives the whole section.

other_edits.operator_offered: could be trimmed to ~150 if the section feels long
other_edits.analyst_recommendation: TRIM to roughly 150 words. The detailed MOND background (a_0 value, McGaugh-Lelli-Schombert, Bekenstein TeVeS, Bullet Cluster) belongs in WIN-052’s detail_evidence (where it is ALREADY present in roughly that level of depth). In the Part 6.13 section, the cross-cutting argument needs MOND treated as a genuine working research program but does not need the full background.

proposed_subsection_html:
6.13 The MOND Grouping — A Category-Error Import

Eleven WINs (WIN-044 through WIN-054) imported in late March 2026 trace, almost item-for-item, to the modified-gravity / MOND community's published list of "anomalies LCDM struggles with" — the Radial Acceleration Relation, the El Gordo cluster, the CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, tidal constituents. Independent convergence on this same list would be striking corroboration. Importing it without a single derivation from disc-cosmology principles is not corroboration — it is borrowing, and the dome's own bounded-firmament commitments (disc radius ~20,015 km; firmament height ~9,086 km) make the derivation route formally impossible at the first dimensional step (data/wins.json:WIN-047,048,052,054,055; dome coordinates.html).

The list, and why list-match matters

The eleven WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-054 in three commits over 32 minutes on the morning of 2026-03-23 are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points. They are the MOND-community talking points: the Radial Acceleration Relation (WIN-052, citing the Mistele 2024 lensing extension at ApJL 969 L3), the El Gordo cluster framed as a "6.2σ ΛCDM impossibility" (WIN-054, the Asencio, Banik & Kroupa 2023 framing), the CMB Axis of Evil (WIN-048), Hubble tension (WIN-047), Cepheid distance-ladder tension (WIN-055), tidal constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 (WIN-045/046/049/050/051), and a custom-derived firmament scaling function bridging two of these (WIN-044, WIN-053). The list is not original to the dome author. It is a published artifact of the modified-gravity research community — Stacy McGaugh maintains it in varying forms on his Triton Station blog; Pavel Kroupa and Indranil Banik in the Bonn group publish its components in their cosmology papers; it circulates in the MOND-curious sphere as the canonical set of anomalies where modified gravity claims its strongest empirical foothold.

The distinction between independent prediction and external import is the deciding question for whether such a list match constitutes evidence. Imagine, hypothetically, that the dome cosmology had been developed in isolation from MOND, working purely from disc-cosmology first principles, and had derived as predictions: a tight relationship between observed and baryonic acceleration in galactic systems; an inability of standard mass-function reasoning to explain a specific cluster mass at a specific redshift; an alignment of CMB low-multipole moments with a special structural axis; a discrepancy between local and CMB-derived expansion measurements. If the resulting dome predictions matched the MOND-curated list, that would be one of the strongest possible forms of corroboration — two independent theoretical programs, working from different principles, converging on the same empirical anomalies. That structure of agreement is what working scientists treat as serious evidence, and it is the kind of move that has historically resolved scientific disputes.

That is not what happened. The dome author did not derive these anomalies from disc-cosmology principles. He imported them, in a single late-March commit sequence, and labeled each one "compatible with ECM" or "consistent with disc cosmology" without specifying any dome-internal mechanism that produces them. There is no derivation chain from the disc radius to RAR's a₀ threshold. There is no dome-mechanical argument for why the El Gordo cluster's mass anomaly should be 6.2σ rather than 4σ or 8σ. There is no firmament-geometric reason the CMB should display a quadrupole-octupole alignment. The compatibility claims are textually identical in structure: "phenomenon X exists, the standard model has trouble with X, the dome model is consistent with X." That is not convergence. It is borrowing.

Why the closed-world commitment forecloses the derivation route

The dome model could in principle attempt the derivation work, and a sympathetic reading would expect it to try. The work has not been attempted, and there is a structural reason: it cannot succeed.

MOND, taken on its own merits, lives at galactic and cosmological scales. The critical acceleration a₀ ≈ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² becomes relevant only at distances of order 10²⁰ meters between the observer and the dominant gravitating mass. The Radial Acceleration Relation is a relationship measured at points along galactic rotation curves, where the radius is tens of kiloparsecs and the relevant baryonic mass distribution is three-dimensional (McGaugh, Lelli & Schombert 2016). The El Gordo cluster's mass is reconstructed from gravitational lensing of background galaxies at z > 1, distances of order 10²⁵ meters. The CMB Axis of Evil is a feature of a microwave background that spans the observable universe.

The dome cosmology, by its own published parameters, has a maximum spatial extent on the order of 10⁷ meters. The disc radius is approximately 20,015 km. The firmament height function H(r) tops out at approximately 8,619 km (data/wins.json:WIN-029 documents the parameter; dome coordinates.html). The local sun is approximately 5,733 km away. The local moon is similarly close. There is no point within the firmament at which gravitational acceleration drops anywhere near a₀, because there are no masses distributed over the spatial extents that would produce such an acceleration profile. There is no measured rotation curve to test RAR against, because stars in the dome model are pinned to the firmament's structure rather than orbiting in galactic potentials. There are no galaxy clusters to lens with, because there are no galaxies to cluster. There is no microwave background, because there is no big bang.

The square-cube scaling problem is the most specific way to see the dimensional incompatibility. Mass distributions in MOND-relevant systems follow volume — the baryonic mass enclosed within radius R is ∫ρ(r) 4πr² dr, the integral of a three-dimensional density profile over a spherical volume. Gravitational acceleration at radius R from such a 3D mass distribution scales as GM(<R)/R². If "galaxies" in the dome model collapse to points or near-points on the two-dimensional firmament, the integrals collapse with them: there is no volume over which to integrate density, no three-dimensional mass profile to compute, no radius dependence for the enclosed-mass function. RAR's predicted relationship between observed and baryonic acceleration becomes formally undefined. The dimensional ratios that distinguish MOND from Newton — accelerations at specific radii produced by specific 3D mass profiles — have no analogue in a model where galaxies are firmament artifacts. Even if a sympathetic dome theorist attempted the derivation, the calculations would fail at the first dimensional step. The required mass distribution does not exist in the model.

The dome model has, in published form, two options for what galaxies are. Either they are real distant gravitating mass-collections — in which case the firmament is not bounded, and the dome model is something other than the dome model — or they are firmament artifacts at sub-30,000-kilometer distances — in which case mainstream measurements of galactic phenomena cannot be marshaled as evidence for the dome, because the measurements were of phenomena the dome cosmology denies exist. The MOND-grouping WINs implicitly take the first option, citing galactic anomalies as real and measured. The dome's bounded-firmament commitments require the second option. The two cannot both be held — which is the structure of self-contradicted, not merely misleading. That is the basis on which WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-052, WIN-054, and WIN-055 are reclassified to Self-Contradicted under sub-rationale "category-error import (MOND grouping)."

The category placement, and the self-own

The dome author's published claim taxonomy has, broadly, three categories visible to readers: WINs (entries in the wins registry, framed as "confirmed predictions"), Predictions / Tracking (entries in the prospective bucket awaiting test-window closure), and Kill-Shot Tests (six side-by-side discriminating tests on killshot.html where the dome and globe make different numerical predictions and one of them matches data). The Kill-Shot tab is structured for genuine binary discriminators — Sydney–Perth rail distance, Polaris elevation at 35.9°N, the August 2026 eclipse magnetic prediction at Ebro, NMP drift rate, SAA decay rate, JFK–LHR aetheric slipstream — each row pairs a dome number with a globe number and a measured outcome. It is not a tab for "things mainstream cosmology can't explain"; it requires a dome-side numerical prediction in the first place.

The dome author does have an internal sub-category for items where the argument is "the standard model has trouble with this." The WIN-048 prose for the CMB Axis of Evil is explicitly labeled by the dome as a "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" — the dome's own framing acknowledges that the argument structure is not "the dome predicts X" but "ΛCDM cannot explain X" (data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence). This sub-category exists. It lives inside the WINs registry rather than in a separate tab, but the rhetorical distinction is one the dome author understands well enough to label.

The self-own is in the placement decision. The MOND-grouping items are flagged as confirmed WINs — entries in the registry tallied as evidence for the dome cosmology, contributing to the headline "69 Confirmed Predictions" scorecard on the home page. Filing WIN-052 as a confirmed WIN means asserting that disc cosmology is confirmed by the Radial Acceleration Relation. Filing WIN-054 as a confirmed WIN means asserting it is confirmed by the El Gordo cluster's mass anomaly. The dome's own "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-framing — at least internally consistent in admitting the argument is "the alternative is in trouble, not my model is right" — was available and was sometimes used (WIN-048 carries the label). It was not used for the rest of the grouping. The author chose the strongest available claim category for material that does not warrant it. That choice, given what was imported, makes the placement itself incoherent: confirmation requires that the dome model contain the phenomenon in question and produce the observed value. Under the dome's own bounded-firmament commitments, none of the five reclassified WINs meet that requirement, and the dome's own sub-category framing already concedes as much for at least one of them.

The deeper lesson

The MOND grouping illustrates a general structural problem with the dome project's approach to scientific corroboration. The author appears to have assumed that any anti-mainstream finding within physics counts as friendly to disc cosmology, because both are in tension with the standard model. The assumption is invalid. The fringe of real science is still real science. It carries empirical commitments. MOND commits to galactic-scale 3D physics. Plasma cosmology commits to electromagnetic structure across cosmic distances. Modified-inertia models commit to their own dimensional architectures. Each fringe research program operates within a framework with specific assumptions that are independently measurable and independently constraining. None of those frameworks accommodate a closed-world firmament cosmology bounded at thirty thousand kilometers. Each, in its own way, requires the universe to be much larger than the dome allows.

This is the deeper observation. The MOND community's anomaly list is not the only place where the dome author could have looked for fringe-but-real scientific support, and the choice he made is in some sense the worst available — not because MOND is bad science (it is not; it is among the most carefully derived of the modified-gravity programs), but because MOND's empirical legitimacy comes precisely from frameworks that operate at scales where galaxies, clusters, the CMB, and cosmological expansion are real entities producing real measurements. The legitimacy that made MOND attractive to import is the same legitimacy that makes the import a self-defeating move. Real-science legitimacy comes from frameworks that operate at scales where the dome cosmology cannot exist, and the dome model cannot have its cosmology be smaller than its sources of legitimacy.

The MOND grouping is not evidence for the dome cosmology. It is the clearest available demonstration that the dome cosmology is incompatible with the empirical commitments of every nearby program in the modified-gravity / alternative-cosmology landscape. The dome author was searching among the most legitimate fringe science available, hoping to find his cosmology's neighbors. The neighborhood does not exist.

changes.old: The dome adopts the standard Hubble constant without derivation. It has no redshift mechanism and no physics that operates at galaxy scales.
changes.new: The dome adopts the standard Hubble constant without derivation; it has no redshift mechanism and no physics at galaxy scales — and its bounded firmament cannot accommodate the galactic-scale referents whose Hubble Law it cites as confirmation. The two cannot both be held.

changes.append_prefix: Framework-level contradiction (bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose redshift the dome adopts as confirmation);

patches.sub_rationale_addition: category-error import (MOND grouping): bounded firmament (~9,086 km) cannot accommodate galactic-scale referents the WIN cites as confirming evidence. See Part 6.13.

changes.old: The ‘LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction’ framing is a false dilemma. The Axis of Evil’s significance has eroded under Planck reanalysis, and a copper firmament would block the CMB observations entirely.
changes.new: The dome’s own copper-firmament commitment (WIN-034) makes CMB observation impossible; the dome cannot simultaneously claim CMB anomalies as confirmation. The detail prose already argues this self-contradiction explicitly; the verdict now matches.

patches.sub_rationale_addition: category-error import (MOND grouping): WIN already invokes "Self-contradiction with WIN-034" inline in its detail prose; verdict label now matches the prose. See Part 6.13.

changes.old: Even if dark matter models fail (debatable), that doesn’t support a 20,000 km dome. The dome has no physics at galaxy scales — this is a false dilemma.
changes.new: The dome’s 20,015 km / 9,086 km bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies whose 1–100 kpc rotation curves the WIN cites as confirmation. RAR has no dome-side derivation, only borrowed MOND symbols. The two commitments cannot both be held.

patches.sub_rationale_addition: category-error import (MOND grouping): the dome’s own bounded firmament cannot accommodate galaxies, yet RAR is cited as "the strongest cosmological confirmation." Existing detail prose already states "the dome model’s physical structure cannot accommodate galaxies"; verdict now matches. See Part 6.13.

changes.old: El Gordo genuinely challenges ΛCDM (we don’t dispute that). But the dome offers no alternative physics — pointing out a problem in one model doesn’t validate a different model that can’t even address the question.
changes.new: El Gordo at z = 0.87 is incompatible with a 9,086 km firmament; either it exists at the claimed distance (falsifying dome geometry) or it is illusory (in which case its measured properties cannot serve as evidence). Detail prose already presents this dilemma; verdict now matches.

patches.sub_rationale_addition: category-error import (MOND grouping): existing detail prose already presents the "inescapable dilemma" structure; verdict now matches the prose. See Part 6.13.

changes.old: Standard candles confirm Hubble’s Law; the dome has no redshift mechanism. The values are textbook physics.
changes.new: Cepheid and SBF distance ladders place galaxies at 10–100 Mpc, orders of magnitude beyond the dome’s 9,086 km firmament. The WIN cites them as confirmation while the dome’s own commitments deny the spatial framework in which the measurements occur.

patches.sub_rationale_addition: category-error import (MOND grouping): Cepheid/SBF anchor galaxies at 10–100 Mpc, "orders of magnitude beyond the dome’s firmament at 9,086 km" per existing detail prose. Verdict now matches. See Part 6.13.

tldr_evidence: The operator surfaced an analytical scaffolding showing eleven WINs (WIN-044–WIN-054) imported in 32 minutes on 2026-03-23 trace item-for-item to the MOND community’s "anomalies LCDM struggles with" list. Five candidates (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) currently sit at Misleading or Not Demonstrated; their existing detail_evidence prose already argues framework-level contradiction with the dome’s bounded firmament. The dome’s killshot.html page is structured for side-by-side discriminating tests, not for "anti-mainstream ammunition" as the operator’s draft assumed.
tldr_verdict: Reclassify all five WINs to Self-Contradicted (Decision 1: ENDORSE), insert as Part 6.13 (Decision 2: Option D), trim TLDR per operator’s offered easy fix and tighten closing line (Decision 3). One BLOCKING revision: rewrite the placement-choice paragraph because the kill-shot-tab framing is factually wrong against the actual tab contents — the dome’s actual taxonomy (WINs / Predictions / Kill-Shot side-by-side / "LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label) yields a tighter self-own argument.
kernel_of_truth: The operator’s analytical scaffolding is exceptionally strong on the substantive points. The commit-history forensics (eleven WINs in 32 minutes, item-for-item match to MOND-community canonical anomaly list), the convergence-vs-import distinction, the closed-world / square-cube argument, and the framework-level contradiction structure are all correct. The category-error reading IS what justifies the SC reclassification. The only revision required is to the placement-choice section’s factual basis (kill-shot tab is not what the draft assumes) — and even that revision arrives at a TIGHTER version of the same observation, using the dome’s actual taxonomy (the "LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label is the more accurate target of the placement-choice critique). The deeper lesson about "fringe of real science still has empirical commitments incompatible with a closed-world dome" is original and rhetorically powerful; it deserves the trim from the universal-quantifier line to the more defensible "the neighborhood does not exist" close. Operator’s methodology call to keep the LLM-import detail in research-note context only is correct — the substantive critique stands without invoking commit metadata.

anticipated_objections.objection: Five verdict flips at once is a lot of motion. Why not stage them — reclassify one or two as a pilot, see how the curmudgeon and any external readers respond, then do the rest?
anticipated_objections.response: Considered and rejected. Staging would create a longer window in which the verdict labels disagree with the prose: WIN-052’s detail says "physical structure cannot accommodate galaxies" but the verdict says "Misleading"; that internal disagreement is itself a goalpost-moving signal that staging would prolong. The five candidates are tightly linked (same import burst, same MOND-list provenance, same framework-level contradiction structure) — reclassifying one without the others would force ad-hoc explanation of why some moved and others didn’t. Single-batch reclassification with a single Part 6.13 audit trail is cleaner.

anticipated_objections.objection: The "category-error import (MOND grouping)" sub_rationale label could be read as ad-hominem about the import process. Should we just call it "framework-level closed-world contradiction" and leave the import provenance out?
anticipated_objections.response: Operator’s methodology note already addresses this: the LLM-import detail (Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 commit footers) is held in research-note context only. The proposed sub_rationale describes the structural pattern (a known external list was imported as a group without per-item derivation) without invoking the LLM-mediation specifically. "Import" here is a content-historical observation — the MOND community curated the list publicly, the dome adopted the list — not a methodology accusation. The phrase is defensible without the LLM detail. Recommend keeping the proposed wording; defer to decider/curmudgeon if a tighter formulation is preferred.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon counter-argument (a) said framework-level contradiction may be IMPLICIT rather than EXPLICIT. Is the firmament actually positioned by the dome as a closed-world boundary, or could the dome author argue it is just the "local" region with broader cosmology unspecified?
anticipated_objections.response: The dome’s own model.html and coordinates.html present the firmament + disc + sun + moon parameters as the model’s entire spatial geometry, not as a "local" sub-region of a broader cosmology. The unified-theory table on the model page lists lambda_A = 4,283 Mpc as an "aetheric wavelength" governing the Hubble-law substitute z = D/lambda_A, which our existing prose (WIN-054 detail_evidence) already flags as exactly equal to c/H_0 = 4,282.7 Mpc to 0.01% precision — i.e., the standard Hubble length relabeled, with no derivation from dome geometry. There is no separate published spatial framework inside which "broader cosmology" could be embedded; the only spatial parameters in the unified theory are the disc/firmament/sun/moon set. A "local region" reading would require the dome author to publish a separate broader spatial model, which has not been done.

anticipated_objections.objection: The kill-shot factual error in the operator’s draft is a serious enough finding that maybe the whole drafted section needs a fundamental rethink, not just a paragraph rewrite.
anticipated_objections.response: No. The operator’s draft has four major sections: list-and-list-match-matters; closed-world-derivation; placement-choice / self-own; deeper-lesson. Three of the four are sound (verified against current dome data and our existing review prose). The kill-shot factual error is localized to the placement-choice section; the rewrite of that section preserves the operator’s underlying insight (the dome chose the strongest claim category for material that does not warrant it) and arrives at a SHARPER version of the argument by anchoring it to the dome’s actual taxonomy. The fix is a paragraph rewrite, not a section-level rethink.
